title: Built two autonomous trading systems from scratch here's what I learned
author: Suitable-End9642
selftext:
**System 1: BTC spot on Coinbase** 

Long-only, spot trading on BTC-USDC. Starting capital deliberately small while I prove edge.

The architecture is 10 launchd agents on a MacBook Pro M4 Pro:

* Market check every 15 min — regime detection, signal evaluation, execution
* Watchdog every 5 min — dead-man switch alerts if market-check goes silent
* 4-hour forced check — API health + daily loss limit
* Daily reset at midnight — P&L summary, Obsidian note
* Sunday research loop — reads recent market-check notes + trade history, generates strategy proposals via Claude Opus
* Monday weekly summary — P&L scorecard

Strategy uses a regime classifier (trending/corrective/ranging/volatile) built off ATR and price slope. Breakout and mean reversion signals weighted by regime. Sentiment layered in via Tavily news cache (2hr TTL to stay inside free tier). No-trade zones for low volatility and mid-range chop.

Risk: 2% per trade, 1.5% stop, breakeven trigger at 2x stop distance, trail activates at 3x.

**System 2: MNQ futures on IBKR** 

Same machine, paper account, Two-sided swing strategy longs and shorts depending on slope-based direction filter.

9 launchd agents, IB Gateway managed headlessly via IBC so it survives reboots and restarts silently at 1am. Python stack with ib\_insync.

The macro layer is what I'm most interested in getting feedback on:

* **Economic calendar blocking** (Finnhub) — hard blocks 30 min before/after NFP, CPI, FOMC, PPI, PCE etc. Active now.
* **VIX logging** (Yahoo Finance) — logging only, planning to use for MR/BO weighting after \~4 weeks of data
* **ES correlation logging** — tracking S&P slope vs MNQ slope every 15 min, planning to use as entry filter after reviewing losers

Philosophy: nothing goes live that didn't run on paper first. Nothing gets added to the filter stack without data backing it.

**What writes where**

Both systems write to an Obsidian vault — market-check notes every 15 min, trade entry/exit notes, daily summaries, weekly summaries. The Sunday research loop (Claude Opus)  reads the last 14 market-check notes + 10 trade notes before proposing anything. Keeps a proposal log so it doesn't repeat the same suggestions.

Happy to answer questions on the architecture/design decision.
